reasoning: Q6 already beats DuckDB by 43% (11ms vs 19.28ms), demonstrating exceptional baseline quality. However, with 2 iterations remaining and clear opportunities for 25-40% combined improvement, we should aggressively pursue explicit AVX-512 SIMD and I/O prefetching to potentially reach ~7-8ms.
focus_areas: Explicit AVX-512 SIMD for filtering and aggregation (replaces compiler auto-vectorization)
I/O prefetching with MADV_WILLNEED for HDD optimization (overlaps I/O with computation)
strategy_notes: Target both CPU-bound and I/O-bound bottlenecks simultaneously. Priority 1: Implement explicit AVX-512 intrinsics (_mm512_loadu_si512, _mm512_cmpge_epi32_mask, _mm512_mask_mul_pd) to replace the compiler auto-vectorization approach (lines 142-152). Process scan loop in blocks of 8 doubles. Priority 2: Add MADV_WILLNEED prefetching after mmap for all 4 column files, and issue targeted prefetch hints for zone-map-qualified ranges before scanning. These optimizations are complementary (CPU and I/O) and should compound to achieve the 25-40% improvement target. The Query Optimizer should implement both simultaneously since they target different subsystems.
notes: Skip lower-priority recommendations (predicate reordering, MAP_POPULATE) for now - they provide marginal gains and can be addressed in iteration 2 if needed. Focus on the highest-impact changes first.
